questions.question: Did the applicant include a Project Narrative?
New applicant with a co-applicant board: Does the application include Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement?
New applicant: Does the application include Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status?
New applicant: Is the applicant a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal, and urban Indian organizations) in the United States?
questions.suggestedResources: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status

questions.question: Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)

Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
questions.suggestedResources: Budget Narrative | Attachment 2: Bylaws | Form 5A: Services Provided | Form 8: Health Center Agreements

questions.question: Does the applicant propose to make services accessible to all? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?
Does the applicant propose a service area announced under this NOFO number? If the answer to Question 9 is "No", select "N/A" for Questions 10 through 13.
questions.suggestedResources: Summary Page | SAAT | Project Abstract | Project Narrative | Form 5B: Service Sites

questions.question: Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in 2026 on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target?
Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT?
Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) from the SAAT?

New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include: Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?

New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.

New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?

New or competing supplement applicant requesting HCH and/or PHPC funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
If the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, is the annual SAC funding request reduced accordingly in the SF-424A and Budget Narrative?
questions.suggestedResources: SF-424A | Budget Narrative | SAAT | Patient and Budget Calculator

questions.question: Is the application complete based on the required documents mentioned in the Completeness and Eligibility Checklist?
Based on BPHC OPPD review, is the applicant eligible?
